metadata.name: WhatIsJsonui
metadata.description: Learn > intro essay. Four H2 sections aimed at a reader who has heard of JsonUI but has not used it: What it is in one sentence / The three pieces / Who it is for / How it differs from SwiftUI / Compose / React alone. ~8-min read.

methods.name: onAppear
methods.description: Seed nextReadLinks from the module-scope NEXT_READ_ENTRIES catalog (two rows: next_installation -> /learn/installation, next_hello_world -> /learn/hello-world) and stamp currentLanguage from StringManager.language. Each row's titleKey / descriptionKey is resolved through StringManager with the learn_what_is_jsonui_ namespace prefix.

methods.name: onNavigate
methods.description: Client-side navigation via router.push(url). Destinations are the spec-mapped URLs enumerated in transitions: /, /learn/installation, /learn/hello-world.

vars.name: nextReadLinks
vars.description: Two closing 'read next' cards pointing at /learn/installation and /learn/hello-world. Seeded by onAppear from the NEXT_READ_ENTRIES static catalog and re-seeded by onToggleLanguage.

transitions.condition: url is a spec-mapped learn URL or /

notes: Third live entry under the Learn tab. Flipping LEARN_ENTRIES row 5 (what-is) to 'live' in HomeViewModel finishes.
Intro essay — no Collection, no custom component beyond CodeBlock + TOC. The reader has not installed anything yet; do not ask them to run commands here — link to /learn/installation for that.
